exam.heroCopy: This rebuilt frontend is organized like a real finals workflow: lock in the structure first, then drill concepts, practice questions, and short review cues.
exam.step1: Start with the summary to rebuild the chapter map.
exam.step2: Use concept cards to sharpen terminology and definitions.
exam.step3: Finish with practice plus review cues to close weak spots.
exam.studyFlowTitle: A smoother way to review this class
exam.subjectStep1: Start with the summary to rebuild the chapter structure.
exam.subjectStep2: Use the concept cards for fast definition and formula recall.
exam.subjectStep3: Finish the practice set before checking the review cues.
exam.conceptHint: Tap to flip and reveal the definition.

instrumentation.heroCopy: This topic page is built for measurement-style electronic design problems: unify hardware, firmware, calibration, and contest workflow so the team can reuse one platform instead of restarting from zero each time.
instrumentation.principleTitle: The sequence to lock before coding hard
instrumentation.principle1: Lock the measurement principle and front-end range before chasing algorithms.
instrumentation.principle2: Lock the sampling clock, reference path, and calibration chain before tuning metrics.
instrumentation.principle3: Lock diagnostics, prompts, and repeatability before mock-contest delivery.
instrumentation.familiesTitle: Four instrumentation tracks that keep coming back
instrumentation.testingTitle: Checks that keep the platform honest
